etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 勿(勿) (wù) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") suggests the action involves the mouth, which is central to the act of a kiss. The character 勿(勿) (wù) provides the sound. There is no direct semantic contribution from 勿(勿) beyond its phonetic value.

examples.chinese: 她给了我一个温柔的吻(她給了我一個溫柔的吻)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěile wǒ yīgè wēnróu de wěn.
examples.english: She gave me a gentle kiss.

examples.chinese: 他们在月光下接吻(他們在月光下接吻)。
初吻总是让人难以忘怀(初吻總是讓人難以忘懷)。
examples.pinyin: Chūwěn zǒng shì ràng rén nányǐ wànghuái.
examples.english: The first kiss is always unforgettable.
